questions.q: On what date did the Constitution of India come into force?
questions.explanation: The Constitution came into effect on 26 January 1950, celebrated each year as Republic Day.

questions.q: On what date was the Constitution of India adopted by the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: The Constitution was adopted on 26 November 1949, now observed as Constitution Day.

questions.q: Who is widely regarded as the chief architect of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar, chairman of the Drafting Committee, is regarded as the chief architect of the Constitution.

questions.q: The day the Constitution came into force is celebrated as which national day?
questions.explanation: 26 January, the day the Constitution commenced, is celebrated as Republic Day.

questions.q: The Constitution declares India to be a sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic:
questions.explanation: The Preamble declares India a sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic republic (socialist and secular added in 1976).

questions.q: The introductory statement of the Constitution outlining its ideals is called the:
questions.explanation: The Preamble is the introductory statement that sets out the guiding ideals of the Constitution.

questions.q: Why was 26 January specifically chosen as the date for the Constitution to take effect?
options: It was the day of Partition
It marked the 1930 Purna Swaraj (Independence Day) pledge
questions.explanation: 26 January honoured the Purna Swaraj declaration first observed as Independence Day in 1930.

questions.q: The Constitution of India is often described as one of the:
options: Longest written constitutions in the world
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution is among the longest and most detailed written constitutions in the world.

questions.q: Fundamental Rights guaranteed by the Constitution include the right to:
options: Property as a fundamental right today
questions.explanation: The right to equality is one of the Fundamental Rights; the right to property was later removed as a fundamental right in 1978.

questions.q: Who became the first President of India when the Constitution came into force in 1950?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad became the first President of the Republic of India on 26 January 1950.

questions.q: The Directive Principles of State Policy in the Constitution were inspired by the constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles of State Policy were borrowed from the Irish Constitution.

questions.q: The idea of Fundamental Rights in the Indian Constitution drew chiefly on which country's example?
questions.explanation: The concept of justiciable Fundamental Rights was largely modelled on the United States Bill of Rights.

questions.q: The parliamentary system of government in the Constitution was modelled mainly on which country?
questions.explanation: India adopted the Westminster-style parliamentary system from the United Kingdom.

questions.q: The original Constitution of 1950 had how many schedules?
questions.explanation: The Constitution originally contained eight schedules; four more were added by later amendments.

questions.q: The Constitution describes India in Article 1 as a 'Union of States' rather than a federation. This wording is attributed to:
options: The influence of the United States
A demand by the princely states
questions.explanation: Ambedkar explained that 'Union of States' stressed that the federation was an indestructible, indissoluble whole.

questions.q: Which feature of the Constitution allows a single citizenship for the whole of India?
questions.explanation: Unlike some federations, the Constitution provides a single, uniform citizenship for all of India.

questions.q: The emergency provisions of the Indian Constitution were largely inspired by which country's framework?
questions.explanation: The emergency provisions drew on the Weimar Constitution of Germany, among other sources.

questions.q: The original Constitution did NOT include Fundamental Duties, which were added later by which amendment?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Duties were inserted by the 42nd Amendment in 1976 on the recommendation of the Swaran Singh Committee.

questions.q: The Constitution provides for an independent judiciary headed by which court?
questions.explanation: The Constitution established the Supreme Court of India as the apex independent judicial body.

questions.q: The concept of a federal structure with a strong centre in the Constitution was influenced notably by which dominion?
questions.explanation: The federal scheme with a strong central government was influenced by the Canadian model.

questions.q: The Preamble was amended in 1976 to add which words, among others?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment of 1976 added the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble.

questions.q: The Constitution originally recognized how many languages in its language schedule?
questions.explanation: The Eighth Schedule originally listed fourteen languages; the number has since grown to twenty-two.

questions.q: Which article of the Constitution is often called the 'heart and soul' by Ambedkar for guaranteeing constitutional remedies?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar called Article 32, the right to constitutional remedies, the 'heart and soul' of the Constitution.

questions.q: The original Constitution provided for the right to property under which articles, later repealed as a fundamental right?
questions.explanation: The right to property under Articles 19(1)(f) and 31 was removed as a fundamental right by the 44th Amendment in 1978.

questions.q: The 'Basic Structure' doctrine, limiting Parliament's power to amend the Constitution, was established in which 1973 case?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court laid down the Basic Structure doctrine in the Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala case in 1973.

questions.q: Which part of the Constitution contains the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles of State Policy are contained in Part IV of the Constitution.

questions.q: The very first amendment to the Constitution, in 1951, mainly addressed restrictions on which right?
options: The right to property in the cities only
Freedom of speech and land reform
questions.explanation: The First Amendment of 1951 imposed reasonable restrictions on free speech and protected land reform laws.

questions.q: Between adoption (26 November 1949) and full commencement (26 January 1950), some provisions took effect earlier. Those articles came into force:
questions.explanation: Some articles, such as those on citizenship and elections, came into force on 26 November 1949, while the rest commenced on 26 January 1950.

questions.q: The Fundamental Duties added in 1976 are listed in which article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties are enumerated in Article 51A, added by the 42nd Amendment in 1976.

questions.q: The concurrent list, allowing both the Centre and States to legislate, was borrowed primarily from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: The idea of a Concurrent List, on which both Union and States can legislate, was drawn from the Australian Constitution.
